meta.originalQuestion: Given the current V3 full-pass evidence, do we have enough confidence to terminate the scientifically invalid cohort, what minimal patches should be applied before the next reset/relaunch, and is a deeper research pass materially useful versus direct implementation?

assignments.scope: Local run-stage semantics and terminal-state policy for exhausted rubric_critic targets
Live telemetry, logs, and state evidence for whether the current cohort is recoverable or should be terminated
Official Convex operational constraints relevant to timeout-heavy apply/error/reconcile mutations

areasOfAnalysis.title: Run-State Semantics
areasOfAnalysis.question: Why do exhausted rubric_critic targets leave runs in running state, and what is the smallest policy/code change to make the failure terminal and operable?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Live Failure Evidence
areasOfAnalysis.question: Do the current logs and run-state rows provide enough confidence to terminate the cohort now instead of waiting for more evidence?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Convex Runtime Constraints
areasOfAnalysis.question: Which Convex runtime limits and design recommendations most likely explain the timeout-heavy mutation pattern, and what architectural adjustments are indicated?

hypothesisConflicts.conflict: Timeout reduction may be secondary to the terminal-policy bug and should not delay the first patch.
